novel_features.name: Stale-dirty rooms
novel_features.description: Find rooms that have been dirty longer than a threshold so you can chase them before arrivals.
novel_features.rationale: Joins dated housekeeping snapshots to compute time-in-status per room — Cloudbeds API exposes status but not duration.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked which rooms are blocking arrivals; native Cloudbeds endpoints can't answer 'how long has this been dirty'.

novel_features.name: Today board
novel_features.description: One-shot front-desk dashboard: arrivals, departures, in-house, unassigned, dirty rooms, today's ADR and occupancy.
novel_features.rationale: Composes five local queries against the synced mirror into one agent-shaped JSON doc — no single Cloudbeds endpoint returns this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this at shift handoff or when an agent is asked 'what's happening at the property right now' — one call instead of five.

novel_features.name: Rate drift
novel_features.description: Show how rates moved over a window, by date, plan, and room type.
novel_features.rationale: Diffs successive dated rate snapshots — Cloudbeds API returns only current rates with no built-in history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked 'did our Tuesday rate hike actually move ADR' — needs persisted rate history the API does not expose.

novel_features.name: Occupancy trend
novel_features.description: Daily occupancy, ADR, and RevPAR series with week-over-week deltas.
novel_features.rationale: Reads dated occupancy snapshots — getDashboard exposes today only; trend requires a local time-series.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for revenue reviews — series and WoW deltas no Cloudbeds endpoint surfaces.

novel_features.name: Source mix
novel_features.description: Bookings, nights, and revenue by source (Booking.com, Expedia, Direct, Airbnb) over a window.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local reservations to sources, aggregates by night and revenue — no native endpoint combines these.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked 'where are bookings coming from' or for partner channel-mix reports.

novel_features.name: Unpaid upcoming
novel_features.description: Reservations arriving in the next N days with non-zero balance.
novel_features.rationale: Joins reservations (arrival window) to payments (balance > 0) — no getReservationsWithUnpaidBalance endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked 'who hasn't paid yet for an upcoming stay' — Cloudbeds web UI requires per-reservation drilldown.

novel_features.name: No-show audit
novel_features.description: No-show rate by source, room type, and lead-time bucket; flags repeat no-show guests.
novel_features.rationale: Filters local reservations by status='no_show' and segments — no native segmentation endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked which sources or segments are bleeding revenue to no-shows.

novel_features.name: Guest history search
novel_features.description: Full-text search across guest name, email, phone, and notes; returns past stays with totals.
novel_features.rationale: FTS5 across guest fields plus a join to reservations for stay history — Cloudbeds getGuestsByFilter cannot do cross-stay totals or notes-text search.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked 'has this guest stayed before' or 'find all guests who booked the honeymoon suite' — FTS hides the verbose getGuestList payload.
novel_features.group: Local search that beats the API

novel_features.name: Mirror reconcile
novel_features.description: Compare local mirror to live API for a sampled window; emit a typed diff.
novel_features.rationale: Pulls both sides and emits added/removed/changed rows — no Cloudbeds endpoint does this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked to verify a sync mirror is current, or to triage a 'guest says they booked but I don't see it' ticket.

novel_features.name: Reservation timeline
novel_features.description: Single-reservation forensic view: status changes, payments, notes, room assignments — chronological.
novel_features.rationale: Joins reservations + payments + housekeeping snapshots + notes by reservationID — no single endpoint returns all four.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked to investigate a reservation dispute or guest complaint — one call instead of four.

novel_features.name: Night audit pack
novel_features.description: End-of-shift bundle: arrivals not checked-in, departures not checked-out, unbalanced folios, payments since last audit.
novel_features.rationale: Composes four local queries into a shift-handoff doc — replaces the Cloudbeds web UI's manual night-audit walk.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for end-of-shift handoff — one agent-shaped doc replaces tabbing across four web-UI screens.

narrative.headline: The Cloudbeds CLI nobody else built — full PMS API surface plus a local SQLite mirror, FTS5 search, dated snapshots, and front-desk transcendence commands no SDK wrapper offers.
narrative.value_prop: Every Cloudbeds reservation, guest, room, rate, and housekeeping record can be synced into a local SQLite store and queried offline with FTS5. On top of the 115-endpoint PMS API surface, this CLI adds front-desk and revenue-management commands — `today`, `housekeeping stale`, `rates drift`, `occupancy trend`, `payments unpaid`, `reservations timeline`, `audit night` — that the official SDKs cannot answer because they require dated snapshots or cross-table joins. Output is agent-native by default: `--json`, `--select`, typed exit codes, `--dry-run` for mutations, and an MCP server tree-mirrored from the Cobra command tree.
narrative.auth_narrative: Cloudbeds API keys start with `cbat_` and never expire unless unused for 30 days. Set `CLOUDBEDS_OAUTH2` in your environment, or run `cloudbeds-pp-cli auth set-token <cbat_…>` to persist the token to `~/.config/cloudbeds-pp-cli/config.toml`. Confirm with `auth status`. The CLI sends the token as `Authorization: Bearer cbat_…`.

quickstart.comment: Verify your API key is set and the API is reachable.
Mirror the last 7 days of reservations into the local store. Add more --resources (get-rooms, get-housekeeping-status, get-rate, get-guest-list) to widen scope.
One-shot front-desk dashboard: arrivals, departures, in-house, unassigned, dirty rooms, today's ADR/occupancy.
Find rooms dirty longer than 4 hours — Cloudbeds API can't answer this without local snapshots.
Reservations arriving in the next 7 days with non-zero balance.

troubleshoots.fix: Set `CLOUDBEDS_OAUTH2=cbat_…` in your environment, or run `cloudbeds-pp-cli auth set-token cbat_…` to persist the token. Run `cloudbeds-pp-cli doctor` to verify.
The CLI's adaptive limiter respects Retry-After automatically; wait or rerun. Cloudbeds caps properties at 5 rps and tech-partners at 10 rps.
Run `cloudbeds-pp-cli sync` first. Transcendence commands read the local mirror — if it's empty they have nothing to compose.

troubleshoots.symptom: API key got blocked after a script bug
troubleshoots.fix: Cloudbeds blocks the key (and your IP) after repeated rate-limit violations. Open a ticket with Cloudbeds developer support — the CLI cannot unblock you.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI any time an agent needs Cloudbeds data shaped for shell pipelines or downstream reasoning — front-desk operations (`today`, `audit night`), housekeeping urgency (`housekeeping stale`), revenue management (`occupancy trend`, `rates drift`, `sources mix`), payment triage (`payments unpaid`), guest investigation (`guests search --history`, `reservations timeline`), and integration debugging (`reconcile`). Pick this CLI over a generic HTTP call when the question requires dated snapshots or cross-table joins, and pick the spec-derived endpoint commands when you need a typed wrapper for a specific RPC.

recipes.title: Front-desk shift handoff in one JSON blob
recipes.explanation: Narrows the today board to the seven numbers a shift supervisor reads to a Slack channel. Without --select agents waste tokens parsing in-house guest objects.

recipes.title: Find the long-staying VIP
recipes.explanation: FTS5 across name, email, phone, notes; returns four agent-readable fields per match instead of the verbose getGuestList payload.

recipes.title: Did Tuesday's rate hike stick?
recipes.explanation: Diffs dated rate snapshots — answer to a question Cloudbeds returns only current rates and cannot answer without local history.

recipes.title: Mirror reconcile in CI
recipes.explanation: Hooks into a CI step that runs after every webhook deploy; pulls fresh reservations from the API, diffs against local mirror, prints added/removed/changed rows.

trigger_phrases: what's happening at the hotel today
